followup_notes.changes_from_prior_cycle: Downgraded Σmν universality claim from 'partially_supported' to 'unsupported' based on codex's valid argument. Slightly lowered overall score from 5.5 to 5.0 reflecting this and the unresolved parameter-count issue. Maintained 'promising' verdict over 'weak_evidence' — the missing text is a bundle quality issue, not a paper quality issue. Added explicit objection to inflated validation count from BBN consistency.
invalid_points_from_other_reviewers: Gemini scoring technical_soundness: 1 and overall: 1/10 based solely on missing bundle text conflates bundle scraping quality with paper quality. The paper is PRL Editors' Suggestion with peer review — a score implying 'likely wrong or not review-ready' is not warranted by a data collection failure.

Gemini's 'fatal' objection severity for the missing text is an overstatement as a paper flaw designation — 'fatal' should be reserved for errors in the paper's reasoning or data, not in the review bundle construction.

Both other reviewers' 'weak_evidence' verdicts are driven almost entirely by the incomplete bundle rather than genuine physics weaknesses. This verdict calibration is appropriate for 'the bundle is too weak to evaluate' but not necessarily for 'the paper itself is weak evidence for its claims.'
valid_points_from_other_reviewers: Codex: The 'same number of free parameters' claim is unauditable from the abstract alone and may hide effective model flexibility from SFRD choice — this is a real concern I had noted but codex sharpens it.
Codex: The universality claim for the Σmν peak is not locally justified by a single-model analysis — upgrading this to 'unsupported' is correct.
Codex: Whether the two SFRDs were fixed pre-analysis or selected post-data-examination is a genuine methodological question that affects statistical interpretation.
All reviewers: The missing numerical values and full article body are a real constraint on review quality, acknowledged consistently.

main_claims.claim: The CCBH model has the same number of free parameters as ΛCDM
main_claims.notes: Cannot audit: comparator name missing in scraped HTML. Codex correctly flags this as unverifiable. Fixing the SFRD to two published observational models is a form of model selection that hides sensitivity to the SFRD choice — the two Σmν values reported for different SFRDs demonstrate this dependence. The cosmological coupling exponent k also needs to be accounted for.
main_claims.paper_location: Abstract (comparator label rendered blank in HTML)

main_claims.claim: The CCBH model accurately recovers the cosmological expansion history and agrees with BBN baryon abundance
main_claims.notes: BBN consistency is expected by construction (CCBH DE production is post-reionization, z < 6; BBN occurs at z ~ 10^8–10^9). Presenting it as a result inflates the apparent validation count. Expansion history recovery is plausible given SFRD-calibrated DE production, but cannot be quantitatively evaluated without the Results section.

main_claims.claim: The CCBH model reduces tension with the local distance ladder
main_claims.notes: Interacting DE models generically modify late-time expansion and can shift H0 inferences. Prior CCBH papers (Croker et al. 2024 JCAP) claimed similar reduction. The degree of tension reduction cannot be evaluated without quantitative results.

main_claims.claim: A positive Σmν is found at 95% confidence for both SFRD choices, consistent with oscillation lower limits
main_claims.notes: Actual Σmν values are blank in the scraped text — a known rendering artifact. The physical mechanism (late-time baryon depletion shifts matter power spectrum normalization, lifting the Σmν posterior off zero) is internally motivated. But the result is entirely conditional on the contested CCBH framework.
main_claims.paper_location: Abstract (numerical values missing from HTML rendering)

main_claims.claim: A peak in Σmν is a general feature of any model converting sufficient matter to dark energy during and after reionization
main_claims.notes: Upgraded from 'partially_supported' to 'unsupported' following codex's valid point: a single-model analysis cannot establish generality across model classes. Different coupling functions, timing, and perturbation structures in generic interacting DE models may or may not produce this feature.

missing_assumptions.assumption: Black holes formed in stellar collapse are exotic compact objects with dark-energy-dense interiors rather than classical GR black holes
missing_assumptions.why_it_matters: Cosmological coupling M ∝ a^k with k > 0 is only possible if BH interiors contain dark-energy-like material. If stellar-collapse remnants are classical Kerr BHs, the entire DE production mechanism fails. This is the central contested assumption in the literature.

missing_assumptions.assumption: The SFRD is treated as a fixed external observational input (zero free parameters) rather than a fitted function or post-hoc selection
missing_assumptions.why_it_matters: If the two bracketing SFRDs were selected after examining cosmological fit quality, this is effectively hidden model flexibility and inflates statistical confidence. The two different Σmν values across SFRD choices indicate non-trivial sensitivity.

missing_assumptions.assumption: The cosmological coupling exponent k is either fixed from theory or from prior data — not a new free parameter in the DESI DR2 fit
missing_assumptions.why_it_matters: If k is a free parameter, the model has more freedom than ΛCDM regardless of SFRD treatment. If fixed from theory, the theoretical derivation of k needs to be clear.

missing_assumptions.assumption: CCBH energy injection does not perturb early-universe observables (CMB anisotropies, recombination) in ways not accounted for
missing_assumptions.paper_location: Abstract (consistency with BBN claimed); Methods/Results absent
missing_assumptions.why_it_matters: Even if bulk CCBH DE production is late-time, Population III star remnants at z > 10 could affect CMB via early DE injection. Presumably handled in the Boltzmann solver modifications, but cannot be verified.

needs_human_spotcheck.question: What are the actual numerical values for Σmν for each SFRD, with confidence intervals, and what is the comparator model referenced in 'same number of free parameters as [blank]'?
needs_human_spotcheck.why_model_cannot_resolve: HTML scraping dropped the mathematical/numerical content. Requires access to the full PRL text.

needs_human_spotcheck.question: Does the paper fix the cosmological coupling exponent k from theory or treat it as a fitted parameter? If fixed, what value and what theoretical derivation?
needs_human_spotcheck.why_model_cannot_resolve: Requires Theory/Methods sections absent from the bundle. Critical for evaluating the parameter-count claim.

needs_human_spotcheck.question: Were the two SFRDs selected before or after examining the cosmological fit quality? Is there a pre-registration or equivalent methodological guarantee?
needs_human_spotcheck.why_model_cannot_resolve: Requires Methods/Appendices and possibly knowledge of the analysis timeline. Post-hoc SFRD selection would inflate statistical confidence.

needs_human_spotcheck.question: Have Faraoni & Rinaldi (PRD 110, 2024) and prior critiques of the CCBH cosmological coupling mechanism been addressed in the Theory section, and is the Croker et al. response considered definitive by the GR community?
needs_human_spotcheck.why_model_cannot_resolve: Requires expert GR assessment of the contested theoretical claims and reading the Theory section, neither of which is available here.

needs_human_spotcheck.question: How much does the H0 tension reduction depend on the SFRD choice, and does either bracketing SFRD bring the tension below 2σ?
needs_human_spotcheck.why_model_cannot_resolve: Requires quantitative results from the absent Results section.

needs_human_spotcheck.question: Have the CCBH modifications to the Boltzmann solver (CAMB/CLASS, accessed via Cobaya) been independently validated or made available for inspection?
needs_human_spotcheck.why_model_cannot_resolve: Requires Methods section and code review; Zenodo release (ref 103) may contain code but cannot be accessed in this review.

novelty_assessment: The paper advances the CCBH program (Croker & Weiner 2019; Croker et al. 2020, 2021, 2022, 2024; Farrah et al. 2023, 2025) with three specific additions: (1) application to DESI DR2 — the most precise BAO dataset to date with 14M targets; (2) a structural prediction of positive Σmν as a consequence of late-time baryon depletion, which is new within this framework; (3) simultaneous claim of H0 tension reduction, Σmν tension resolution, and dark energy model selection within a single physically motivated model. Matter-to-DE interacting models are an established subfield (Farrar & Peebles 2004; Amendola 2000; Yang et al. 2018; Nunes et al. 2022), so the CCBH flavor's novelty lies primarily in its astrophysical grounding through the SFRD. The Σmν prediction mechanism appears genuinely new within this framework. Overall novelty is moderate-to-high as an observational application, incremental as theory.
score_rationale: Significance (7): Simultaneous resolution of H0 tension, negative Σmν problem, and dark energy model selection using DESI DR2 — the highest-precision BAO dataset — would be impactful if correct. The Σmν prediction mechanism is physically motivated and structurally novel within the CCBH framework. Novelty (6): Application to DESI DR2 with new neutrino mass prediction is substantive but incremental over prior CCBH work; the theoretical framework is contested and predates this paper. Technical soundness (4): Penalized for (a) contested CCBH cosmological coupling mechanism with unresolved GR critiques, (b) full analysis unverifiable from bundle, (c) unauditable parameter-count claim, (d) universality claim unjustified from single-model analysis. The PRL Editors' Suggestion status provides a meaningful upward prior. Clarity (4): Only abstract accessible; numerical values blank in HTML; comparator model name blank. Reviewer confidence (4): Abstract-only review with contested theoretical foundations limits confidence substantially. Overall 5.0: The paper is interesting and potentially significant but rests on a contested theoretical mechanism and cannot be fully audited from this bundle. The verdict is 'promising' rather than 'weak_evidence' because the limitation is in the bundle, not demonstrably in the paper — the distinction matters for calibration.
summary: PRL Editors' Suggestion (PRL 135, 081003, August 2025) by the DESI Collaboration combining DESI DR2 BAO + CMB to test cosmologically coupled black holes (CCBHs) as a dark energy production mechanism tied to the cosmic SFRD. Key claimed results: recovery of expansion history, BBN consistency, reduced Hubble tension, and positive summed neutrino mass Σmν at 95% confidence. The full article body (Theory/Methods/Results/Discussion sections) is absent from the review bundle — only the abstract and reference list are accessible. All three cycle-1 reviewers flag this limitation. The absent text is a bundle/scraper quality issue, not a paper flaw, and should not be conflated with paper quality in the verdict scoring. Assessments remain abstract-level and prior-literature-grounded.

technical_objections.evidence: Mass growth M ∝ a^k requires black holes to be exotic compact objects (ECOs) with dark-energy-like interiors. Standard Kerr/Schwarzschild BHs in cosmological backgrounds do not exhibit this coupling. Faraoni & Rinaldi (PRD 110, 2024) and Faraoni & Jacques (2007) examined cosmological coupling solutions critically. Community consensus on whether realistic astrophysical BHs can exhibit the required coupling at astrophysically significant k values has not been reached.
technical_objections.objection: The theoretical basis for cosmological coupling of black hole masses is non-standard and actively contested
technical_objections.paper_location: Theory section (absent from bundle); prior work Croker & Weiner 2019, Croker et al. 2022, Faraoni & Rinaldi 2024

technical_objections.evidence: Only abstract and reference list present; Theory, Methods, Results, Discussion sections listed in outline but content not available. Numerical Σmν values appear blank in abstract HTML. This is a bundle quality issue, not evidence of paper error — the paper is PRL peer-reviewed with Editors' Suggestion status.
technical_objections.objection: Full article body is absent from the review bundle, making quantitative claims unverifiable

technical_objections.evidence: Fixing the SFRD to two bracketing observational functions hides SFRD model dependence — the two different Σmν results demonstrate this. The coupling exponent k must also be counted. If the SFRD is treated as a fixed observational input, the claim may be technically true but obscures real model sensitivity.
technical_objections.objection: The 'same number of free parameters as ΛCDM' claim is unauditable and likely misleading

technical_objections.evidence: CCBH DE production is driven by stellar collapse, which peaks at z ~ 2 post-reionization. BBN occurs at z ~ 10^8–10^9, many orders of magnitude earlier. The CCBH mechanism cannot affect BBN baryon abundance by construction. Listing this as a result inflates the number of independent validations.
technical_objections.objection: BBN consistency is presented as a model validation but is expected by construction

technical_objections.evidence: Different matter-to-DE interacting models (Amendola 2000; Pourtsidou et al. 2013; Nunes et al. 2022) have varied coupling functions and perturbation structures. Showing the effect in the CCBH scenario does not establish it as universal across model classes without a more general theoretical argument.
technical_objections.objection: The universality claim for the Σmν peak cannot be established from a single-model analysis
